food_factory_deep_pain_1_desc: Food factories must pass HACCP, GMP, and FSSC22000 certifications. Traditional manual processes make it hard to consistently meet Critical Control Points (CCP), and audit failures lead to shutdowns or fines.
food_factory_deep_pain_2_desc: Food safety incidents require factories to complete batch tracing within 2 hours. Paper records and manual entry are inefficient and error-prone, making it impossible to quickly locate batches and root causes.
food_factory_deep_pain_3_desc: Large-scale food production requires three-shift operations. Manual operator fatigue significantly degrades night-shift quality. Equipment failures lack early warning — a single shutdown can cost tens of thousands.
food_factory_deep_pain_4_desc: Traditional open-flame stoves have only 30%-40% thermal efficiency. Steam and electricity consumption accounts for 20%-30% of operating costs, yet most factories lack effective energy monitoring.
food_factory_deep_pain_desc: HACCP compliance pressure, batch traceability gaps, 24/7 line stability, rising energy costs — these pain points are slowing down your food factory's profitability and expansion.

sr_pains_title: Common Pain Points for Small Restaurant Startups
sr_pains_subtitle: The hardest part isn't opening — it's keeping the kitchen running smoothly, the flavor consistent, and the service fast.
sr_pain1_title: Chefs Are Expensive and Hard to Retain
sr_pain2_body: A 10–15 m² kitchen has to fit prep tables, stoves, and a packing area. Traditional gas ranges turn the whole kitchen into an oven in summer — staff can barely stand it.
sr_pain3_body: Regulars come for your signature taste. Today Chef A cooks it, tomorrow Chef B — the regular takes one bite and asks "Did you change chefs?" That's how you lose customers.
sr_pain4_body: At 11:30, delivery and dine-in orders flood in simultaneously. Two woks can't keep up. Customers wait 20 minutes with no food — they cancel the order, and bad reviews arrive the next day.
sr_pain5_title: Kitchen Like a Furnace, Monthly Bills Shocking?
sr_pain5_body: Open-flame kitchens hit 40°C+ in summer. Staff can only endure 2 hours. Gas cylinders need replacing several times a month, electricity bills keep climbing — that's how profits get eaten.
sr_pain6_title: Equipment Breaks = Business Stops, No One to Fix It?
sr_pain6_body: At noon the stove breaks down. The next batch is prepped but can't cook. You call the supplier — they say 3–5 days. How do you serve for the next 3 days?
sr_solution_title: How to Survive the Lunch Rush in a 15 m² Kitchen?
sr_solution_desc: The answer isn't hiring more people — it's letting equipment do the work reliably. No chef needed, no large kitchen required. Set up the equipment and start serving.
sr_feat1_body: Countertop wok, induction cooker, stewing machine — fried rice, char kway teow, fried chicken, braised pork, all done on the countertop. No floor space needed.
sr_feat2_body: Nasi Goreng, Pad Thai, fried rice, braised pork — 1,000+ recipes with preset heat and stir-fry timing. Same flavor regardless of who operates.
sr_feat3_body: Temperature and timing are machine-controlled. No more burnt or undercooked dishes. New operators are independent in 30 minutes.
sr_feat4_body: Start with the XC60. As daily orders grow, upgrade to larger capacity models — no need to invest everything upfront. Add a unit when orders increase.
sr_equipment_title: New to the Business — Which Equipment Handles Peak Best?
sr_eq1_body: The first unit for any small restaurant. Fits on a countertop, serves fast — standard dishes ready in ~3 minutes. During peak, alternate between two woks.
sr_eq2_body: Soups, braised dishes, pre-prepared meals. Stew before service and hold in the insulated unit — serve directly at lunch without occupying the wok.
sr_eq3_body: Fried chicken, french fries, snacks — if your menu has fried items, you need this. Precise temperature, reliable batches every time.
sr_eq6_body: Only needed at 300+ daily orders. Large single-batch capacity — fewer flips, dozens more orders during peak.
sr_eq4_body: All ingredients cut and prepped 10 minutes before peak. The wok handles cooking — one person can run the whole show.
sr_eq5_body: Consider this when daily orders exceed 150. Larger capacity means fewer batches during peak — dozens more orders per shift.
sr_scenarios_title: Four Store Types, Four Kitchen Configurations
sr_scenario_1_desc: No dine-in, delivery only. The kitchen just needs to cook fast and pack fast. One wok + one insulated server, 1–2 people is enough.
sr_scenario_2_desc: Dine-in and delivery at the same time — the kitchen must cook for both. Wok for mains, fryer for sides, stewing pot for soups — three lines running independently.
sr_scenario_3_desc: One kitchen running multiple brands — every square meter must produce. Equipment must switch items fast: one machine goes from stir-fried noodles to fried rice in 5 minutes.
sr_scenario_4_desc: Built on 1–2 signature dishes — the key is consistency. Equipment locks in the heat and rhythm so regulars keep coming back for your flavor.

news_1_body:
TRANSLATE: At the recently concluded Asia Food Expo 2026 in Bangkok, Thailand, {brand} made a stunning debut with its all-new 5th generation smart cooking robot, becoming the center of attention throughout the event. The expo attracted over 2,000 foodservice companies from Southeast Asia, South Asia, and the Middle East.

The 5th generation smart cooking robot represents a comprehensive upgrade over its predecessors, featuring an all-new AI cooking engine that supports over 800 recipes spanning Chinese, Southeast Asian, and Western cuisines. Equipped with a precision temperature control system and adaptive stir-frying technology, the device achieves a cooking consistency rate of 99.5%, far exceeding the industry average.

The {brand} booth was bustling throughout the expo, with representatives from over 200 restaurant brands across Thailand, Vietnam, Malaysia, and Indonesia experiencing the device firsthand. Multiple restaurant chain groups expressed partnership intentions on the spot, with the first batch of 50 units expected to be delivered and deployed within six months.

{brand} CEO stated in a media interview: "Our goal is not just to provide a smart device, but to create a complete intelligent kitchen solution for foodservice businesses. Through the deep integration of AI technology and IoT platforms, {brand} helps clients achieve standardization and automation across the entire workflow from prep to plating, significantly reducing labor costs while ensuring the high quality and consistency of every dish."

Additionally, {brand} unveiled its new cloud management platform at the expo, supporting remote equipment monitoring, automated maintenance alerts, and one-click recipe updates, further enhancing operational efficiency and intelligence in commercial kitchens.
news_1_summary: TRANSLATE: {brand} unveiled its 5th-generation smart cooking robot at the expo, achieving 99.5% cooking consistency and 70% labor cost reduction, attracting interest from 200+ F&B brands across Southeast Asia.
news_1_title: TRANSLATE: {brand} Smart Cooking Robot Debuts at 2026 Asia Food Expo
news_2_body:
TRANSLATE: A leading Southeast Asian restaurant chain has announced the large-scale deployment of {brand} automated cooking lines across all 86 outlets, marking a milestone in the commercialization of kitchen automation in the region. The project represents a total investment exceeding USD 12 million, the largest single order in {brand}'s Southeast Asian market history.

The chain operates three major business segments — quick service, casual dining, and fine dining — across Thailand, Vietnam, and Cambodia. With the introduction of {brand} automated cooking lines, the brand has achieved fully automated production from ingredient preparation and automatic cooking to finished product packaging at its central kitchens, increasing daily meal output per outlet by 3x while reducing labor costs by 65%.

The project is being rolled out in three phases: Phase 1 involves equipment installation and commissioning at 30 flagship stores, Phase 2 covers the remaining 56 outlets, and Phase 3 will launch the {brand} cloud management platform for unified scheduling and data analytics across the entire network.

The chain's Operations Director commented: "{brand}'s equipment has performed exceptionally well during the trial period. The high consistency in food quality has significantly boosted customer satisfaction. More importantly, the {brand} team provided comprehensive after-sales service and technical support, giving us complete confidence throughout the deployment process."

{brand}'s Southeast Asia Regional Director revealed that the successful implementation of this project has laid a solid foundation for the company's expansion across the region. Multiple restaurant companies from various countries are currently in discussions for similar large-scale automation projects, with another wave of orders expected in the second half of 2026.
news_2_summary: TRANSLATE: The brand installed {brand} cooking lines across 86 outlets, enabling centralized kitchen standardized production and tripling daily per-store meal output.
news_2_title: TRANSLATE: Southeast Asian Chain Deploys {brand} Fully Automated Cooking Lines
news_3_body:
TRANSLATE: Following a rigorous six-month audit and on-site inspection process, all {brand} products have successfully achieved ISO 9001:2015 international quality management system certification and European Union CE safety compliance certification, marking a significant milestone in the company's quality control and international market access capabilities.

The ISO 9001:2015 certification requires companies to meet international standards across all aspects of quality management, including R&D design, manufacturing, supply chain management, and after-sales service. The auditing body gave high praise to {brand}'s quality management system, particularly recognizing the company's rigorous approach to raw material traceability, standardized production process controls, and pre-delivery product inspections.

The CE certification is a mandatory market access requirement for the European Union, involving strict testing across multiple dimensions including electrical safety, mechanical safety, and electromagnetic compatibility. All {brand} smart cooking equipment successfully passed every test category, demonstrating that the products have achieved world-class standards in safety and reliability.

{brand}'s Quality Management Director stated: "Obtaining dual certification is an important achievement of our long-standing quality-first strategy. We have invested substantial resources in building our quality management system, with strict standards and procedures at every step from supplier screening to final product delivery. These certifications are not only a recognition of our existing work but also a driving force for continuous improvement."

{brand} is currently advancing applications for US UL certification and Japan JIS certification, expected to be completed by the end of 2026. Upon completion, the company will have a comprehensive certification system covering all major global markets, providing strong support for further international market expansion.
news_3_summary: TRANSLATE: After rigorous auditing, {brand}'s full product line passed international quality management and EU safety compliance certifications, providing customers worldwide with more reliable product assurance.
news_3_title: TRANSLATE: {brand} Earns ISO 9001:2015 & CE Dual Certification
